messages.content:
Which of the following passages most strongly shows Abuela's lack of familiarity with New Jersey?
© A. "Right up to the time when we're supposed to pick up the old lady at the airport, my mother is telling me stories about how hard times were for la familia on la
isla, and how la abuela worked night and day to support them after their father died of a heart attack."
B. "It is January, two feet of snow on the ground, and she's wearing a shawl over a thick black dress."
• C. "It's like she had a spot picked out for some unknown reason, and although it's the most inconvenient seat in the house, that's where she has to sit."
D. "She is praying loud, in Spanish, and singing hymns at the top of her creaky voice."
B. "It is January, two feet of snow on the ground, and she's wearing a shawl over a thick black dress."

What does the following passage most strongly show about the father's perspective of his daughter?
My father decides that he should stay home with my mother and that I should escort la abuela to church. He tells me this on Saturday night as I'm getting ready to go to the mall with my friends
"No way," | say.
1 go for the car keys on the kitchen table: he usually leaves them thele for me on Friday and Saturday night. He beats me to them.
No way," he says, pocketing them and grinning at me
A. The father believes the daughter needs to learn to drive more safely.
B. The father believes the daughter should be punished for being disagreeable.
C C. The father believes the daughter needs gentle encouragement to do the right thing
D. The father believes the daughter should learn to make decisions more quickly.
C. The father believes the daughter needs gentle encouragement to do the right thing.

Which is most strongly a similarity between the narrator and Abuela?
A. Both the narrator and Abuela wear clothes that make them appear small in stature.
B. Both the narrator and Abuela enjoy telling jokes to the family to make them laugh.
C. Both the narrator and Abuela cover their faces with their hands when embarrassed.
D. Both the narrator and Abuela walk with small steps to get to the church on Sunday.
D. Both the narrator and Abuela walk with small steps to get to the church on Sunday.

Which passage best identifies the source of the narrator's guilt at the end of the story?
[ A. "Once home, she refuses to let my mother buy her a coat because it's a waste of money for the two weeks she'll be in el Polo Norte, as she calls New Jersey; the
North Pole."
• B. "I do have a responsibility to Sandra and Anita, who don't drive yet."
• C. "When we get to the apartment, my parents are at the kitchen table, where my mother is trying to eat some soup."
D. 'Your grandmother says to tell you that of all the hurtful things you can do to a person, the worst is to make them feel as if they are worth nothing."
D. "Your grandmother says to tell you that of all the hurtful things you can do to a person, the worst is to make them feel as if they are worth nothing."

Which inference about the narrator's feelings toward Abuela is best supported by the text?
A. She is hoping to show Abuela around the city
B. She is excited to introduce Abuela to friends.
© C. She is uncertain how to keep Abuela safe.
D. She is ashamed to be seen with Abuela.
C. She is uncertain how to keep Abuela safe.

Which sentence from the short story most strongly supports the answer to Question 5?
• A. "I try to walk far behind them in public so that no one will think we're together."
• B. "There is a Harley-Davidson fashion show at Brookline Square that we cannot miss."
© C. "I warm it up for Abuela as instructed by my parents, and drive it to the front of our building."
• D. "She doesn't speak to me on the way home, and she doesn't let me help her walk, even though she almost falls a couple time."
C. "I warm it up for Abuela as instructed by my parents, and drive it to the front of our building."

Which sentence from the short story most strongly supports the answer to Question 5?
• A. "I try to walk far behind them in public so that no one will think we're together."
• B. "There is a Harley-Davidson fashion show at Brookline Square that we cannot miss."
© C. "I warm it up for Abuela as instructed by my parents, and drive it to the front of our building."
• D. "She doesn't speak to me on the way home, and she doesn't let me help her walk, even though she almost falls a couple time."
D. "She doesn't speak to me on the way home, and she doesn't let me help her walk, even though she almost falls a couple times."

Which of the following best describes how the narrator's mother feels at the conclusion of the story?
A. The mother is upset by the way her daughter has treated Abuela.
• B. The mother is hopeful that her daughter will get to know Abuela better.
C. The mother is confused by the story her daughter tells her about Abuela.
D. The mother is grateful to show her daughter photographs of Abuela as a child.
A. The mother is upset by the way her daughter has treated Abuela.

Which sentence from the excerpt most strongly supports the answer to Question 7?
• A. "Then she goes to her room."
© B. "But it sounds lame, even to my own ears."
[ C. "She comes out with tears in her eyes."
[ D. "So I just sit there not saying anything"
C. "She comes out with tears in her eyes."

Sort the following events in chronological order.
Available Options ( 4 of 4 )
Abuela wears a shawl in the middle of an East Coast winter.
T
# The narrator learns the meaning of 'zero' (as it is used in the title of the story).
The narrator does not help her abuela when she is lost.
The narrator is bribed into taking Abuela to church.

1. Abuela wears a shawl in the middle of an East Coast winter.
2. The narrator learns the meaning of 'zero' (as it is used in the title of the story).
3. The narrator is bribed into taking Abuela to church.
4. The narrator does not help her abuela when she is lost.
Describe Constancia's relationship with Abuela prior to her grandmother's visit to New Jersey. Cite details from the text to support your response.

Constancia's relationship with Abuela prior to her grandmother's visit to New Jersey was not very close. Constancia regarded her grandmother as someone who did not speak English and who did not understand the world in which she lived. The story describes how Constancia purposely walks far behind her grandmother on the street so no one will see them together. For example, when Abuela arrives for her visit, the narrator's mother asks if she wants to speak to Constancia on the phone, but Abuela simply shakes her head and says, "She won't understand me anyway" (paragraph 45). Additionally, when the narrator agrees to take Abuela to church, she notes that she has never been to church with Abuela before, suggesting a distance in their relationship.
resumelo en un texto corto

"Mi Abuela" es una historia corta que muestra la relación distante entre Constancia y su abuela antes de que ésta la visitara en Nueva Jersey. A medida que la historia se desenvuelve, el lector se entera de que Constancia no se siente cómoda con su abuela debido a las barreras lingüísticas y culturales, y se avergüenza de ser vista en público con ella. Pero a medida que la historia avanza, Constancia comienza a comprender la importancia de la conexión familiar y se siente culpable por haber tratado a su abuela con insensibilidad. Al final, la relación entre las dos mejora y Constancia aprende una valiosa lección sobre el valor de la familia.
resumelo mas y traducelo al ingles

"Mi Abuela" es una historia breve que muestra la relación distante entre Constancia y su abuela antes de que ésta la visitara en Nueva Jersey. A medida que la historia se desenvuelve, se revela que Constancia no se siente cómoda con su abuela debido a las barreras culturales y lingüísticas que existen entre ellas. En particular, Constancia se avergüenza de ser vista en público con su abuela y presupone que no tienen nada en común. Sin embargo, cuando abuela visita la ciudad, Constancia comienza a comprender la importancia de la conexión familiar y se siente culpable por haber tratado a su abuela con insensibilidad. Al final, la relación entre las dos mejora y Constancia aprende una valiosa lección sobre el valor de la familia y la necesidad de superar las diferencias culturales para encontrar un terreno común.
resumelo mas

"Mi Abuela" es una historia que trata sobre la relación entre una nieta y su abuela. La nieta, Constancia, se siente incómoda con su abuela debido a las diferencias culturales y lingüísticas. Sin embargo, cuando su abuela visita la ciudad, Constancia aprende la importancia de la familia y se siente culpable por haber tratado a su abuela con insensibilidad. Al final, la relación entre las dos mejora y Constancia aprende una valiosa lección sobre el valor de la conexión familiar y la necesidad de superar las diferencias para encontrar un terreno común.
